title: New Financial Year, Same Spending Pattern: The Capital Allocation Reset Most Owners Skip
linkedin:
FY26 just closed. FY27 spending has probably already started, and there's a good chance it's a repeat of last year's pattern with a small adjustment either way.

That's not a criticism. It's what happens when the people running the business are also the people flat out running it. Building a spending plan from scratch every year takes time nobody has spare in the first week of July.

Here's the cost of that shortcut. Almost every dollar an SME spends beyond the basics lands in one of four buckets: people, stock, fit-out and equipment, or marketing. Each one usually gets reviewed on its own, by whoever owns that part of the business, rather than side by side against what it actually returned.

Reviewed separately, every bucket can look reasonable. Reviewed together, against the return each one generated over the year just closed, the picture usually shifts. A marketing channel that stopped converting eighteen months ago is still funded at the same level as the one carrying the business. A role that made sense at a smaller size still exists because removing it feels riskier than keeping it.

The first fortnight of July is the cleanest window all year to run this comparison. FY26 is closed and won't move again. FY27 hasn't built its own momentum yet. For a short window, you can look plainly at where capital went against what it generated, before this year's pattern locks itself back in by default.

The businesses that use this window well aren't running complicated spreadsheets. They're asking one question for each bucket: did this earn its place again this year?

Full piece on the ProfitPulse blog.

#FractionalCFO #FinancialStrategy #AustralianBusiness
facebook:
Quick question for the business owners in our feed: when did you last actually decide where this year's money goes, versus just carrying over what you spent last year with a small tweak?

Most of us do the second one. It's not laziness, it's just what happens when you're the one running the business and setting its budget.

The catch is that everything from marketing to staffing to stock gets funded again by default, whether or not it's still earning its place. FY26 just closed, which makes this the cleanest week all year to actually check.

When did you last review the four big spending buckets side by side rather than one at a time?

#SMEFinance
google:
A quick note for business owners as the new financial year gets underway.

Most businesses set this year's spending by carrying over last year's pattern with a small adjustment up or down. It is not a bad instinct, it is just what happens when you are running the business and setting its budget at the same time.

The cost is that spending on people, stock, fit-out and marketing keeps getting funded at roughly the same level each year, whether or not each one is still earning its place. A marketing channel that stopped working eighteen months ago can keep getting the same budget as the one actually driving the business, simply because nobody compared the four side by side.

Right now is the easiest time all year to make that comparison. Last financial year's numbers are final and will not shift again, and this year's spending has not yet built its own momentum. For business owners across Brisbane, the Gold Coast, and the wider east coast, a short review this month of what each spending category actually returned tends to matter more to the year ahead than most of what is on the to-do list right now.

Read the full piece on the ProfitPulse blog.
